purpose: Identify publicly available data on stored/parked/grounded aircraft as teardown candidates for MRO parts arbitrage

united_states.name: Pinal Airpark (Pinal County Airpark)
united_states.capacity: 400+ aircraft on 500 acres (200 acres for storage)
united_states.aircraft_types: Boeing 747, 777, A320, EMB-145, wide-body and narrow-body mix
united_states.data_availability: No public tail-number list. Must contact directly or use satellite/spotter databases.
united_states.arbitrage_relevance: HIGH - Ascent tears down 70-100 aircraft/year, parts flow into USM market. Watching new arrivals = early signal of parts supply.

united_states.name: Southern California Logistics Airport (SCLA)
united_states.aircraft_types: Boeing 737, 747, A380, wide variety. Southwest stored 50x 737-700s here (2020). Qantas stored A380 fleet here.
united_states.data_availability: No public inventory. Spotter community active. Planespotters.net and FlightRadar24 show aircraft at KVCV.
united_states.arbitrage_relevance: HIGH - Second largest commercial boneyard worldwide. Major teardown and MRO activity.

united_states.name: Mojave Air & Space Port
united_states.data_availability: Very restricted. No photography allowed on flightline. Email approval required from Business Class Aviation.
united_states.arbitrage_relevance: MEDIUM - Many secretive aerospace R&D operations. Less transparency on teardown activity.

united_states.name: Roswell International Air Center (RIAC)
united_states.data_availability: No public tail-number list. Spotter community reports available.
united_states.arbitrage_relevance: HIGH - Active teardown facility. Dry climate (4-8% humidity) ideal for long-term storage. COVID surge brought large volumes.

united_states.name: Phoenix Goodyear Airport
united_states.data_availability: Limited public data. Covered by airplaneboneyards.com.

united_states.name: Davis-Monthan AFB / AMARG
united_states.data_availability: PUBLIC - FOIA inventory released monthly. Searchable at amarcexperience.com
united_states.notes: Military aircraft only. Parts reclamation serves DoD. Not directly relevant to commercial MRO arbitrage, but military surplus parts sometimes cross over.
united_states.arbitrage_relevance: LOW for commercial, but military surplus parts can overlap (engines, avionics, fasteners).

international.name: Asia Pacific Aircraft Storage (APAS)
international.capacity: Up to 200 aircraft (100 hectare site), handles all types including A380
international.data_availability: No public inventory list. Limited to spotter reports and press releases.
international.arbitrage_relevance: HIGH for Asia-Pacific market. Aircraft leaving here often go to teardown elsewhere.

international.name: TARMAC Aerosave - Teruel
international.capacity: 140 aircraft + 20 engines at Teruel. 270 total across 3 sites (incl. Tarbes and Toulouse, France).
international.aircraft_types: A380, A340, 747-400, various. As of Oct 2024: 63 aircraft including 8 A380, 16 A340, 5 747-400.
international.data_availability: Occasional press releases with aircraft counts by type. No public tail-number list.
international.arbitrage_relevance: HIGH - Europe's largest storage/recycling facility. A380/A340 teardowns generate large component supply.

free_or_freemium.name: Planespotters.net
key_features: Production list search with URL filters (manufacturer, type, fleet, status)
Complete aircraft history from production to scrap
Delivery and scrap events tracked within days
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: HIGH - Can filter all stored/scrapped aircraft by type. Direct signal for parts supply entering market.

free_or_freemium.name: Airfleets.net
free_or_freemium.coverage: All major commercial aircraft manufacturers (Airbus, Boeing, Embraer, Bombardier, etc.)
key_features: Dedicated stored fleet pages per airline (e.g., /flottecie/KLM-stored-b737.htm)
free_or_freemium.caveats: Stored status may be stale - aircraft listed as stored may already be scrapped. Small airlines/aircraft not tracked.
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: HIGH - Free, structured data on stored fleets by airline and type. Good for monitoring fleet transitions.

free_or_freemium.name: FAA Releasable Aircraft Database

key_files.MASTER.txt: All currently registered aircraft with status codes
key_files.DEREG.txt: All deregistered aircraft (scrapped, exported, destroyed, etc.)

free_or_freemium.privacy_warning: FAA considering restricting some registration data (FAA-2025-0638) per FAA Reauthorization Act of 2024.
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: CRITICAL - DEREG.txt is the single best free signal for aircraft being scrapped/parted out. Daily updates. Bulk downloadable. Cross-reference with parts marketplaces to detect new supply entering market.

free_or_freemium.name: FAA Aircraft Registry Online Lookup
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: MEDIUM - Good for individual aircraft verification but not bulk analysis.

free_or_freemium.name: AMARG/AMARC Experience Database
key_features: Monthly FOIA inventory updates from 309th AMARG
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: LOW for commercial MRO. Military parts have separate certification requirements.

free_or_freemium.name: OpenSky Network
free_or_freemium.coverage: Live ADS-B tracking, aircraft metadata database
key_features: Can detect aircraft that stopped transmitting
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: MEDIUM - Can build 'last seen' detection for aircraft going dark (= entering storage). Academic historical data useful for research.

free_or_freemium.name: AirplaneBoneyards.com
free_or_freemium.coverage: Comprehensive directory of all major boneyards worldwide with interactive map
key_features: Facility profiles with capacity, operators, photos
European, Australian, and US facility coverage
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: LOW-MEDIUM - Reference/directory only. No tail numbers or real-time data.

free_or_freemium.name: AviationDB
free_or_freemium.coverage: All current, deregistered, and reserved US civil aircraft (FAA data mirror)
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: MEDIUM - Web interface for FAA data.

free_or_freemium.name: EUROCONTROL Grounded Aircraft Dashboard
free_or_freemium.coverage: Active vs inactive aircraft across EUROCONTROL area (Europe)
key_features: Interactive dashboard of grounded/parked aircraft by state, airport, operator
Historical data from COVID pandemic onwards
free_or_freemium.arbitrage_value: MEDIUM - European fleet parking trends. Aggregate data, not tail-number level.

paid_professional.name: ch-aviation
key_features: Filter by status: Active, Inactive, Scrapped/Written Off
Drilldown to all aircraft stored at a specific airport
paid_professional.pricing: Subscription-based. Custom pricing. Contact for quote.
paid_professional.arbitrage_value: VERY HIGH - Best structured database for filtering stored/scrapped aircraft globally. Status + location + dates = actionable teardown intelligence.

paid_professional.name: Cirium (Ascend)
paid_professional.coverage: 220,000+ commercial aircraft, 840+ aircraft types, 170+ engine types, 300+ datapoints per aircraft
key_features: Complete aircraft history at serial number level
paid_professional.pricing: Enterprise pricing. Evaluation accounts available. Contact for quote.
paid_professional.arbitrage_value: VERY HIGH - Industry gold standard. Valuations + fleet status = can model teardown economics. Expensive but most comprehensive.

paid_professional.name: IBA Group
paid_professional.coverage: Real-time fleet data with historic values and transaction history
key_features: Live fleet data: orders, deliveries, lease ends, ownership events
Parked/stored as percentage of regional fleet
paid_professional.arbitrage_value: VERY HIGH - Lease-end data = leading indicator of aircraft entering teardown pipeline. Valuation data critical for parts-out economics.

paid_professional.name: Aviation Week Fleet Discovery
paid_professional.coverage: 220,000+ commercial and business aviation aircraft, 840+ types, 170+ engine types
key_features: Complete aircraft history at serial number level
paid_professional.arbitrage_value: HIGH - Comprehensive but overlaps with Cirium. Good for cross-referencing.

paid_professional.name: FlightRadar24 API
key_features: Can detect 'last flight' by checking flight history gaps
paid_professional.free_tier: Basic web tracking free. API requires paid subscription.
paid_professional.arbitrage_value: HIGH - Build automated 'last flight detection' system. Aircraft that stop flying = entering storage = future teardown candidates.

paid_professional.name: FlightAware AeroAPI / Firehose
paid_professional.type: Paid (usage-based for AeroAPI, enterprise for Firehose)
key_features: Firehose: streaming TCP feed for enterprise
paid_professional.pricing: AeroAPI: usage-based (per query). Firehose: enterprise pricing (contact for quote).
paid_professional.arbitrage_value: HIGH - Same 'last flight detection' capability as FR24. Historical data replay for backtesting storage detection algorithms.

teardown_companies_to_monitor.description: These companies acquire aircraft for teardown/parts-out. Monitoring their acquisitions = advance signal of parts entering USM market.

companies.name: GA Telesis
companies.stats: 275+ airframe disassemblies, 600+ engine disassemblies, billion-dollar ecosystem
companies.notes: Recently announced A321 disassembly from Magnetic Leasing acquisition.

companies.name: AerSale
companies.stats: Invested $100M in feedstock in 2025. Wins ~1 in 10 deals bid on.
companies.notes: Publicly traded (ASLE). Earnings calls reveal feedstock acquisition data. 'Hypercompetitive' feedstock market.

companies.name: Ascent Aviation Services
companies.stats: 70-100 aircraft teardowns/year. 10 parallel teardown lines. 500-acre facility.
companies.notes: New $70M investment in wide-body hangars (Dec 2025). Key facility to watch.

companies.name: TARMAC Aerosave
companies.notes: Airbus/Safran/Suez JV. Europe's dominant teardown/recycling player.

companies.name: AAR Corp
companies.notes: Major USM distributor and MRO provider.

tier_1_free_immediate.description: Start here. Free data sources that can be operationalized immediately.

actions.action: Download daily. Parse new deregistrations. Filter by aircraft type relevant to target parts. Cross-reference with parts price database.
actions.signal: Aircraft deregistered = parts entering market within 3-12 months. Price of those parts will drop.

actions.action: Scrape production lists filtered by 'stored' status. Monitor changes weekly. Track which aircraft types are accumulating in storage.
actions.signal: Rising stored count for a type = growing parts supply = future price pressure.

actions.action: Scrape airline stored fleet pages. Track fleet transitions from active to stored.
actions.signal: Airlines parking specific types = those types becoming teardown candidates.

tier_2_low_cost.description: Moderate investment for significantly better data.

actions.action: Use stored/scrapped status filters to build comprehensive global picture. Export data. Set up monitoring.
actions.signal: Most actionable single database for stored aircraft intelligence.

actions.action: Build 'last flight detection' algorithm. Monitor specific tail numbers. Alert when aircraft stops flying for >30 days.
actions.signal: Early detection of aircraft entering storage before formal status change.

actions.action: Use free academic access for historical analysis. Build models of storage-to-teardown timelines.
actions.signal: Validate timing assumptions for parts supply entering market.

actions.action: Full fleet tracking + valuations. Model teardown economics per aircraft.
actions.signal: When aircraft value drops below parts-out value = teardown imminent.

actions.action: Lease-end tracking. When leases expire on older types, those aircraft often go to teardown.
actions.signal: Lease expiry data = 12-24 month advance signal of parts supply.

market_context.feedstock_competition: AerSale invested $100M in feedstock in 2025 but only wins 1 in 10 deals. Market is 'hypercompetitive'.
market_context.supply_demand_mismatch: New aircraft deliveries lag demand until 2031-2034. This keeps older aircraft flying longer, REDUCING teardown feedstock and INCREASING USM parts prices.
market_context.market_size: Aircraft parts market: $1.03 trillion in 2025, growing at 5.62% CAGR to $1.51T by 2032
market_context.key_insight: The combination of slow new deliveries + aging fleets + competitive feedstock market = USM parts prices remain elevated. Arbitrage opportunity exists in early detection of teardown feedstock before prices adjust.
